prose: Fukui reactivity calculation on formaldehyde (4 atoms: O, C, H, H) using DFT with B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier standard) in gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The calculation computed Fukui function indices by finite-difference comparison of Mulliken charges between the neutral molecule and its cation (+1, doublet) and anion (−1, doublet) states using PySCF. The most electrophilic site (highest f⁺) is the carbon atom with f⁺ = 0.7084; the most nucleophilic site (highest f⁻) is the oxygen atom with f⁻ = 0.4674. The dual descriptor for carbon is +0.5959, indicating it is the primary electrophilic center in the molecule. Charge conservation checks passed (Σ(f⁺) = 1.0000, Σ(f⁻) = 1.0000), confirming convergence of the ±1 states. A Fukui visualization plot was generated. No warnings reported; integrity status: trustworthy.
